xBRIEFInfo.author: agent:cursor
xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF for disabling the minutes-interval input when a chord voice is looping

plan.title: Grey out the chord minutes-interval input when loop is on

narratives.Overview: UX polish for the chords section: when a chord voice's 'loop' toggle is ON, its per-voice minutes-interval control has no effect (the loop re-triggers on the BPM grid, not the minutes interval), so it should be visibly disabled/greyed out to avoid confusing the user. When loop is OFF, the interval control behaves exactly as today. This is a presentational change in the ChordsPanel; no scheduling/engine/logic changes.
narratives.Requirements: FR-1: In the chords panel, when a voice has loop enabled, its minutes-interval input (the 'every N minutes' control) is rendered disabled and visually greyed out (e.g. reduced opacity / disabled styling consistent with the app). FR-2: When loop is disabled, the interval control is fully enabled and unchanged from current behavior. FR-3: Toggling loop updates the interval control's enabled/greyed state immediately (reactive to the setting). FR-4: The underlying stored interval value is preserved while disabled (do not clear/reset it) so turning loop back off restores the prior interval. Optionally add an unobtrusive hint (e.g. title/aria or helper text) explaining the interval is controlled by tempo while looping — optional, keep it subtle. NFR-1: Accessibility — use the input's `disabled` attribute (not just CSS) so it's non-interactive and announced as disabled. NFR-2: Match existing ChordsPanel/globals.css styling conventions; no new dependencies. NFR-3: Purely presentational — no changes to chords.ts scheduling, chords-engine.ts, or synths. NFR-4: Update the co-located ChordsPanel test to cover the disabled-when-looping behavior; typecheck, lint, full test suite, and production build pass.
narratives.Assumptions: A-1: The per-voice `loop` boolean and the minutes-interval control already exist in ChordsPanel after the loop-mode feature (PR #123). A-2: ChordsPanel is the presentational component; if it is hookless/stateless, the disabled state is derived from the passed-in settings for that voice. A-3: 'Greyed out' = the app's standard disabled affordance (disabled attribute + muted styling).

items.title: src/components/ChordsPanel.tsx — disable + grey the minutes-interval input when the voice's loop setting is on (preserve stored value)

narrative.Traces: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3, FR-4, NFR-1, NFR-3

items.title: src/app/globals.css — disabled/greyed styling for the interval control if needed (match existing conventions)
Update ChordsPanel test to assert the interval input is disabled when looping and enabled otherwise; verify gates/build
